untranslatable_reason: Signal requires implied volatility (IV) and historical volatility (HV) indicators that are not in the allowed indicator list. The core logic depends on: (1) IV-to-HV spread calculation and comparison to standard deviation thresholds, (2) persistence duration tracking of the divergence, and (3) realized volatility trend direction assessment. Only hvol_20 (historical volatility) is available; implied volatility and IV-HV spread calculations are not supported. Additionally, the CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction requires separate LONG and SHORT entry/exit logic that cannot be determined without thresholds and market regime context from the original signal parameters.
translation_notes: This is a volatility mean-reversion signal from Natenberg's framework. It requires exotic derivatives volatility metrics (IV vs HV) that are not available in the backtest engine's indicator library. The signal also contains multiple [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] placeholders that require domain expertise to calibrate. To implement this signal, the engine would need to support: (1) implied_volatility indicator, (2) iv_hv_spread calculation, (3) realized_vol_trend or similar directional volatility indicator, and (4) duration/persistence tracking for multi-day conditions.
